And then um yeah, like how are you guys leveraging that?
Are you leveraging it for like price changes?

So we're very old school when it comes to that.

Um We find that information is not always accurate, actually, 99% of the time.
Um, but not alone on that, I'll tell you that have these calls all day and not the first and definitely not the last person.
Sure.

Um They call the properties themselves and get that information.
So it's a lot of manual work for our site team.
So um we generally, when we're trying to talk about price changes, we have them update their market survey, we discuss it internally and the prices are set.
Ok?
And how often are your site teams going out and getting this data twice a month on the 10th and the 25th?
Ok, twice a month.
And then you are leveraging that for pricing discussions.
How often are you changing uh the rates of your units only when one if they're highly occupied?
Um So let's say they hit 98%.
We typically increase prices or if they're, you know, behind on um or, you know, trending, we go, we look 90 days out.
So if they can start falling below 94 we start having a con a conversation about putting and implementing specials.

We're not really one to set or change G pr um or I should say decrease, it's more of an increase only situation we would rather put in monthly specials or one time, you know, concessions in place before we'll just market rate.
Ok.
That makes sense.
Um ok.

what are your thoughts on that?

So I'm I'm curious if like the combination of, you know, there being gaps in the data is that the primary reason of like taking the call today, I guess essentially what I'm trying to ask is internally, has there been conversations around streamlining this collection uh of market data process or is this kind of, you don't know what you don't know and you just want to get some more information.

I've wore many hats.
I've had six different titles.
Um, I took the director of marketing role last year while still overseeing a portfolio myself.

I wanna give, you know, the site teams back time because it seems like nobody has time and let's be real, nobody is reliable these days for actually doing their job on site.
And I, I mean that in a nice way, they're just so busy and, you know, their attention span is very short.
I would agree with you on that.

Totally.
Yeah, so ii I totally get kind of like what you're saying on that.
Um And I appreciate the transparency for sure.
I don't mean that in a way of like, hey, are you gonna buy this or not?

How do we get our hands on it?
How do we, you know, fix this issue?
So I'm trying to be a problem solver right now.

But um essentially apartment IQ can fully automate and streamline this process for you.
Uh We can essentially take out the whole human element of it.
So with us, you are getting a live data feed um of data around not only your property but each specific comp set.
So the way it works is if you sign up with us, you give us a list of your properties, you give us a list of any of the comps you're tracking for each and we build out the shell of the data for you.
And we can give you uh data points from a high level property perspective.

What was the total increase or decrease?
We track concessions daily as well.
So really the biggest thing for us is you can log into the platform every day, know that you're looking at the most up to date accurate information because it refreshes every 24 hours.
Ultimately allowing you to leverage this data and see where your units are positioned every single day within each specific comp set.
So instead of having to go through a bunch of, you know, an entire market of data, an entire sub market of data where some of these assets aren't true competitors to yours.
They're different classes, things like that.
This is all specifically tailored to your properties, your comps and your business.
